guide_review_response.summary:
Iteration 1 of Stage 2 Executor Agent (EA) instance 6 comprehension-gate review. Verdict: APPROVED on iteration 1 -- the third HIGH-risk-stage iter-1 APPROVAL in this Stage 2 chain (after EA-4 iter-1 APPROVED + EA-5 iter-1 APPROVED). Pickup verification (V0a-V0l) all 12/12 PASS, independently re-verified by Guide instance 6 against current disk state: HEAD = f863a22 with parents 9277d61 + d0c6df1 verbatim; git status --porcelain reports 33 entries (22 M + 2 D + 9 ??) with the +1 M observed on tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py EXACTLY as Guide-#6 v5 verification correction C1 predicted; stash@{0} timestamp 20260424_013002 byte-identical; state.json fleet_paused=true with last_updated_by= "copilot_agent" + last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" preserved; pytest tools/ 282 passed (per EA-6 V0f); grep `LiveVikunjaClient\(\s*\)` returns exactly 6 production callers verbatim matching the g6-ea6_n1 list with line numbers (54, 84, 169, 123, 93, 42) -- I re-ran this grep myself; output matches EA-6's V0k breakdown exactly.
All 4 v5 documentation corrections (C1-C4) are restated VERBATIM with explicit EA-6 acknowledgment + V0 verification reference per defect (C1 cross-references V0c+V0l; C2 cross-references V0b; C3 cross-references V0b verbatim match; C4 references HEAD f863a22 throughout the gate). All 5 v4 documentation corrections (D1-D5) carried forward as SATISFIED-AS-AUDIT-HISTORY with brief restatement (D2 33-ack count + D1 g5-N mapping + D3 cleaner.py substantive + D4 f1 body-rejection-condition + D5 phantom decorator).
All 41 binding acks restated VERBATIM with proper source_file attributes per ack group (decomposition: 9 critical_carryovers + 3 g3 + 4 i1 + 7 g4 + 4 g5-1..g5-4 + 2 g5-5/g5-6 + 4 g5-7..g5-10 + 4 g6-ea5 SATISFIED + 4 g6-ea6 FORWARD-ACTIVE = 41). f1 = g5-5 (NOT enumerated separately). Math correct.
Comprehension summary parts a/b/c/d/e/f are comprehensive: part_a_objective restates the full Stage 2 trajectory through 2.10; part_b_work_plan enumerates D2-completion (FIRST per g6-ea6_n1) → D1 → 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → 2.8 → 2.9 → 2.10 with priority labels and deliverable specifics; part_c_files_to_modify lists the 6 D2-completion targets with line numbers + variable names + 4 NEW V matrix test files + NOT-modified list; part_d_verification_strategy details per-deliverable verification including 2.10 STATUS append-only diff inspection; part_e_risks lists 10 descriptive labels with reference anchors per g4-6 compound pattern; part_f_rollback_acknowledgment is verbatim from the inherited Stage 2 XML <rollback> mandate. Stop discipline correct (no D2-completion edits, no commits, no state mutation, no resume/pause cycles). Zero open questions to Guide -- EA-6 is fully ready to proceed.
NOTABLE INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT (worth surfacing): EA-6 caught a documentation gap in Guide-#6's init pointer + v5 XML BEFORE/AFTER pattern. The init pointer's BEFORE pattern was "live = LiveVikunjaClient()" -- but 3 of the 6 callers use the local variable name "client" (credential_rotation_reminder.py:54, daily_digest.py:169, weekly_summary.py:123) while the other 3 use "live" (welcome_back_digest.py:84, dashboard_maintainer.py:93, gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py:42). Independent disk verification by EA-6 surfaced this 3-3 split. EA-6 explicitly committed in g6-ea6_n1 ea_6_acknowledgment to "preserve existing local naming and modify only the constructor invocation" -- a sharp catch that prevents a subtle regression where blindly applying the BEFORE/AFTER pattern would have changed variable names in 3 of 6 callers and potentially broken downstream local references. This is exactly the LA's "mature not minimal" mandate (p3 + p16 + dn14 + dn20) working as intended. Mature engineering with appropriate disk-verification discipline.
All 8 axes from GUIDE_PROMPT.xml v2.1 ea_comprehension_gate_review pass: objective_match, work_item_coverage, file_scope, verification_strategy, risk_awareness, rollback_awareness, pre_flight_discipline, hard_rules. Zero substantive corrections required. EA-6 may proceed to D2-completion 6-caller closure work immediately.

independent_disk_verification_of_ea6_cla.intro: Per LA's "mature not minimal" mandate (p3 + p16) and dn14 (do NOT trust EA reports without disk verification), Guide instance 6 independently re-ran the V0 pickup commands at review time to confirm no drift between EA-6's observed state and live disk state.
independent_disk_verification_of_ea6_cla.check: Guide-6 observed: f863a22 (HEAD), 9277d61, d0c6df1. EA-6 V0b observed: f863a22, 9277d61, d0c6df1 (with full commit messages verbatim). EXACT MATCH.

Guide-6 independently ran `git status --porcelain | wc -l` → 33 entries. Guide-6 independently ran `git status --porcelain | grep '^ M' | wc -l` → 22 M. Guide-6 independently listed all 22 M files; the list contains `tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py` at position 8 in the alphabetical order. EA-6 V0c observed: 33 entries, 22 M files in the same alphabetical order, with fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py at position 8 + annotation "← +1 M v4→v5 (per C1; D2 PARTIAL conditional load_config + D3 enabler update_task)". EXACT MATCH on count, list, ordering, and +1 attribution.
Guide-6 observed: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002. EA-6 V0d observed: identical timestamp. EXACT MATCH. Stash byte-identical.

Guide-6 independently ran `grep -rn 'LiveVikunjaClient\(\s*\)' tools/`. Returned 6 production hits (matching EA-6's V0k breakdown verbatim) + 1 README.md example hit (out-of-scope; not production): tools/fleet_observability/credential_rotation_reminder.py:54: client = LiveVikunjaClient() tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py:169: client = LiveVikunjaClient() tools/fleet_observability/dashboard_maintainer.py:93: live = LiveVikunjaClient() tools/fleet_observability/weekly_summary.py:123: client = LiveVikunjaClient() tools/fleet_observability/welcome_back_digest.py:84: live = LiveVikunjaClient() tools/gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py:42: live = LiveVikunjaClient() tools/gate_stale_cleaner/README.md:28: report = run_sweep(client=LiveVikunjaClient()) ← example only EA-6 V0k observed the same 6 production hits with line numbers. EXACT MATCH. The 4-vs-2 variable naming split (`client` x4 + `live` x2) is correct; EA-6 caught this in g6-ea6_n1 ea_6_acknowledgment (see commendations below).

Cross-checked C1, C2, C3, C4 in EA-6 gate against the corresponding entries in the Guide-#6 v5 verification file + EA-6 init pointer's <v5_documentation_corrections_required> block. Verbatim match on v4_claimed text, correct_value text, and ea_6_acknowledgment text reflects the correction without paraphrase or omission. The v5 XML's count_delta misattribution to GUIDE_PROMPT.xml is explicitly rejected in EA-6's C1 acknowledgment (line 112 of EA-6 gate). All 4 v5 corrections fully internalized.
D1-D5 v4 corrections carried forward as SATISFIED-AS-AUDIT-HISTORY (lines 138-150 of EA-6 gate). Brief restatement is acceptable since EA-5 iter-1 gate restated them verbatim and the corrections are now in the audit chain.

EA-6 §total block (line 239 of gate) claims 9 + 3 + 4 + 7 + 4 + 2 + 4 + 4 + 4 = 41. Arithmetic: 9+3=12; 12+4=16; 16+7=23; 23+4=27; 27+2=29; 29+4=33; 33+4=37; 37+4=41. CORRECT. f1 = g5-5 (not separately enumerated) is consistent with established chain.

Each ack group cites a source_file attribute pointing to the correct chain-of-custody file. 9 critical_carryovers + 3 g3 + 4 i1 cite 2.4-RESPONSE-EA_COMPREHENSION_GATE_ITERATION_2_STAGE2.xml. 7 g4 cite both that file + 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_TO_EA_INITIALIZATION_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml. 4 g5-1..g5-4 cite 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml. 2 g5-5/g5-6 cite 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_F1_DISPOSITION_OPTION_A.xml. 4 g5-7..g5-10 cite 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_4_F1_AND_2.5_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS.xml. 4 g6-ea5 cite 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_INTER_DELIVERABLE_REVIEW_EA_5_D2_INCOMPLETE_V5_AUTHORIZED.xml. 4 g6-ea6 cite 2.6-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_6_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_ITEM_2.6_D2_COMPLETION.xml. ALL sources match the documented chain-of-custody.

axis_assessment.axis: EA-6 part_a_objective restates Stage 2 from D2-completion → D1 → 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → 2.8 → 2.9 → 2.10 with full sub-deliverable detail. Branch chore/platform-extraction, single-commit-at-2.9 head discipline (current f863a22 will become parent of Stage-2 commit), fleet pause invariance, stash byte-identity (timestamp 20260424_013002), Vikunja stopped, scheduled tasks Disabled, no merge to main, no governance edits all explicitly captured. D4 DIVERGE-APPROVED + D3 CLEAN retention from v5 explicitly noted; no rebar opening. Aligns with Stage 2 XML <objective> + <scope> + v5 work_remaining block.

All Stage 2 remaining items enumerated with priorities: D2-completion FIRST (per g6-ea6_n1 CRITICAL), D1 SECOND, 2.7 THIRD, 2.7.v2 FOURTH, 2.8 FIFTH, 2.9 SIXTH, 2.10 SEVENTH. No items dropped or merged. Sub-deliverables enumerated: - D2-completion: 6 callers with line numbers + variable name annotations - D1: 12 grep patterns enumerated verbatim - 2.7.v2: V4-V7 NEW tests with descriptions - 2.10: 10 required inclusions enumerated (A11 + g5-7 + g5-8 + g5-9 + g5-10 + f1 + encoding regression + 2.5.v2 evidence + v4 D2 footnote + v5 C1-C4 footnote)

part_c_files_to_modify is explicit: - 6 D2-completion targets (verbatim list with line numbers) - 4 NEW V matrix test files (proposed names: test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py, test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py, test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py, test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py) - STATUS.md append-only at 2.10 NOT-modified list explicit (governance docs, docs/platform_separation/* except STATUS, tools/_vikunja_client.py chokepoint, tools/_project_context.py).
Path-confusion discipline maintained throughout: tools/_vikunja_client.py (chokepoint, Stage 2.1) vs tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py (legacy, Item 2.6 D2 target) consistently distinguished per dn18 + g5-6.

V0a-V0l pickup verification COMPLETE (12/12 PASS, independently re-verified by Guide instance 6). Per-deliverable verification strategy detailed for each remaining item: - D2-completion: pytest re-run + grep zero-hits + manual diff inspection - D1: 12-pattern grep tabulation + per-hit triage + escalation rule for non-zero findings outside scope - 2.7: pytest tools/ ≥282 + V1-V7 closure + per-item V8 - 2.7.v2: 4 NEW V matrix tests pass with parametric inputs (BlarAI vs second project overlay) - 2.8: $BLARAI_PID==3 hard-gate python -c oneliner; STOP on drift - 2.9: git diff --name-only shows ALL 33+ entries + new V matrix tests + STATUS edit; pyproject.toml present; commit message matches v5 §work_remaining §2.9 template - 2.10: STATUS.md diff shows append-only addition (no edits to prior entries); all 10 required inclusions present

part_f_rollback_acknowledgment restates verbatim from inherited template: "If post-item-2.9 verification fails (V1-V7 closure or per-item V8), rollback is `git reset --hard HEAD~1` on chore/platform-extraction (level-1 per RECOVERY.md; branch is local, not pushed). Branch is preserved for audit per copilot-instructions preservation_rule. Fleet stays disabled -- no runtime impact from rollback." Mandatory acknowledgment for HIGH-risk Stage 2 per GUIDE_PROMPT.xml axis honored.

meta §fleet_state_invariants_preserved + V0e verified fleet_paused=true with preserved metadata. EA-6 explicitly committed in <stop_discipline> that no state.resume_fleet/pause_fleet calls this session. Function-name discipline embedded in ack n=2 + g6-ea6 prohibitions referenced in the gate.

Master Plan §D.1 (Vikunja project_id HARD RULE) explicitly anchored throughout the ack restatement (n=6: list_tasks/search_tasks MUST receive int project_id; bool rejected; g5-5: chokepoint helper rejection condition extended). Single-commit at 2.9 with pyproject.toml MUST in commit (i1-3). No-merge-to-main during Stage 2 (n=3). stash@{0} untouched (n=4). No governance doc edits (n=5). All 41 binding acks restated VERBATIM with explicit source_file attributes per ack group. dn18 path-confusion prohibition honored throughout. The 4 v5 documentation corrections (C1-C4) restated VERBATIM with explicit ea_6_acknowledgment per defect; 5 v4 corrections (D1-D5) carried forward as SATISFIED-AUDIT-HISTORY.

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6 caught a documentation gap in Guide-#6's init pointer + v5 XML BEFORE/AFTER pattern via independent disk verification. The init pointer used "live = LiveVikunjaClient()" as the BEFORE pattern; in reality 4 of the 6 production callers use the local variable name "client" instead of "live" (credential_rotation_reminder.py:54, daily_digest.py:169, weekly_summary.py:123 use `client`; welcome_back_digest.py:84, dashboard_maintainer.py:93, gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py:42 use `live`). EA-6 explicitly committed in g6-ea6_n1 ea_6_acknowledgment to "preserve existing local naming and modify only the constructor invocation." This is a sharp catch that prevents a subtle regression where blindly applying the BEFORE/AFTER pattern would have changed variable names in 4 of 6 callers and potentially broken downstream local references. This is the LA's "mature not minimal" mandate (p3 + p16 + dn14 + dn20) working as intended.

commendation.topic: Comprehensive 41-ack verbatim restatement with source citations

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: Each ack group cites the correct chain-of-custody source_file attribute. Math is correct (9 + 3 + 4 + 7 + 4 + 2 + 4 + 4 + 4 = 41). Status flags applied per init pointer table. SATISFIED acks restated verbatim as audit history; FORWARD-ACTIVE acks restated verbatim with commitment to enforcement. f1 = g5-5 (not enumerated separately) is consistent with the established chain.

EA-6 added ea_6_observation/ea_6_acknowledgment annotations on top of the verbatim g6-ea5 acks (lines 209-222), explicitly cross-referencing V0g (dup cleanup), V0j (D2 conditional), V0k (6 zero-arg callers), and V0b (docs-only commit) to confirm EA-5's claimed satisfactions reproduce on disk. This is mature audit-chain maintenance.

All 10 risks use compound labels with reference anchors (g5-4-, w12-, w13-, C6-, C2-, i1-4-, w3-, dn18-, dn19-, c1-) per g4-6 + Guide-#5 standing reminder. No bare numeric labels. Severity tags (HIGH/MED/LOW) applied appropriately.

g6-ea6_n4 ea_6_acknowledgment recognizes 5 clean breakpoints (D2-completion-close, D1-close, 2.7-close, 2.7.v2-close, 2.8-close-pre-commit) and accepts the C4 self- reference limitation as inherent to mid-stage handoff XMLs. Mature handoff discipline pre-allocated.
EA-6 closed iteration-1 with no open questions to Guide. The plan is comprehensive and the verification framework is in place. EA-6 is fully ready to proceed.

standing_reminders_for_ea_6_during_execu.intro: The reminders below are NOT required corrections and require no re-emission. They are clarifying notes addressed to EA-6 during execution of the approved work plan.

reminder.topic: Variable name preservation across 6 callers (3-3 split)

standing_reminders_for_ea_6_during_execu.reminder: You correctly identified the `client` vs `live` split (3 of each). When applying the BEFORE/AFTER pattern, modify ONLY the constructor invocation argument list. Keep the assignee variable name as it appears in each caller verbatim. Grouped by variable name: client x3: credential_rotation_reminder.py:54, daily_digest.py:169, weekly_summary.py:123 live x3: welcome_back_digest.py:84, dashboard_maintainer.py:93, gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py:42 Per-caller AFTER text (verbatim assignee + new constructor args): credential_rotation_reminder.py:54: client = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) daily_digest.py:169: client = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) weekly_summary.py:123: client = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) welcome_back_digest.py:84: live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) dashboard_maintainer.py:93: live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py:42: live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"])

Per dn14/dn20 + p3+p16 mature-not-minimal mandate: independently re-read each of the 6 caller files end-to-end before applying the AFTER pattern. Verify that `cfg` is indeed resolved via prior ctx-aware load_config call BEFORE the LiveVikunjaClient constructor invocation in each file. If any caller does NOT have `cfg` resolved pre-constructor (or resolves it differently), STOP and report -- the BEFORE/AFTER pattern assumes `cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]` is available at the constructor call site.

Your V0k correctly excluded the README.md example hit (tools/gate_stale_cleaner/README.md:28: `report = run_sweep(client=LiveVikunjaClient())`). That is a documentation example, not production code, and is out-of-scope for the 6-caller closure. Post-D2-completion grep verification expects 0 production hits but the README hit may persist (it is a doc-string-style example showing API surface, not executable code). Acceptable to leave the README as-is; flag if you decide to update it for documentation consistency in 2.10.

reminder.topic: V matrix V4-V7 test file naming

standing_reminders_for_ea_6_during_execu.reminder: Your proposed V matrix test file names (test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py, test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py, test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py, test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py) are descriptive and follow Python naming conventions. Acceptable. If you find during 2.7.v2 authoring that a different naming feels more pytest-discoverable or test-organization-friendly, you have latitude to adjust -- the test names are not bound by any external citation.

The 2.10 STATUS append must include the full cumulative set of inclusions as enumerated in your part_b_work_plan §item_2_10_status_append. Cross-reference the verbatim footnote text in your §v5_documentation_corrections_acknowledged §documented_in_2_10_status_append_per_init_pointer block AND the v4 D2 footnote text from EA-5 init pointer §v4_documentation_corrections_required §documented_in_2_10_status_append_per_g5_10_corollary block (carried forward in your §v4_documentation_corrections_acknowledged D2 entry). Both footnotes are required at 2.10.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
PROCEED -- you may begin Item 2.6 D2-completion 6-caller closure work immediately. Per your committed g6-ea6_n1 ea_6_acknowledgment: (1) Pre-edit: re-read each of the 6 caller files end-to-end (per dn14/dn20 + p3+p16 mature-not-minimal mandate) to verify `cfg` resolution context before applying the BEFORE/AFTER pattern. (2) Apply the AFTER pattern preserving local variable naming (`client` vs `live`). (3) Run pytest tools/ --tb=short -q; expect 282 passed (no V matrix tests target this code path; baseline holds). (4) Run grep `LiveVikunjaClient\(\s*\)` tools/; expect 0 production hits (README.md example may persist; out-of-scope per r3 reminder). (5) Ack g5-4 transitions FORWARD-ACTIVE → SATISFIED.
After D2-completion lands successfully, proceed to D1 extended grep verification net (12 patterns; mechanical work; ~30-60 min). Then Item 2.7 (pytest gate + V1-V7 + V8). Then Item 2.7.v2 (V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring; AST-driven probe pattern at scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 is precedent for V5). Then Item 2.8 ($BLARAI_PID==3 hard gate; STOP+report on drift per g4-5). Then Item 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit folding ALL work; pyproject.toml MUST be in commit per i1-3; commit template per v5 §work_remaining §2.9). Then Item 2.10 (append-only STATUS append per g5-10 + corollary + v4 D2 footnote + v5 C1-C4 footnote).
If context exhaustion threatens at any clean breakpoint before Item 2.9 (D2-completion- close, D1-close, 2.7-close, 2.7.v2-close, 2.8-close-pre-commit), emit v6 mid-stage handoff XML following v1/v2/v3/v4/v5 schema (docs-only commit, ONE file in diff). NO partial-refactor commits. NO emergency truncated 2.9 commits.
Standing reminders applicable throughout: single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only; fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet -- AttributeError); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; STATUS append-only at item 2.10. On Stage 2 closure (item 2.10 done), emit final closing handoff to LA per the closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close block in your initiation pointer.
